data.narrative:
Quandela is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Valerian Giesz, Pascale Senellart, and Niccolo Somaschi. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies across public and private markets: 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

The peer-median approach is inapplicable to Quandela's valuation. The analysis identified only one same-stage Series B company within the quantum computing universe, with zero adjacent-stage comparables and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling short of the three-datapoint minimum for reliable peer triangulation. Relying on a broader all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological error given the heterogeneous maturity profiles, technology architectures, and revenue trajectories across the wider peer set.

Without sufficient comparable data to execute triangulation, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range. Credible pricing would require either: (1) expansion of the peer universe beyond quantum computing to adjacent deep-tech infrastructure sectors; (2) direct access to transaction multiples or revenue/EBITDA metrics from recent Series B quantum rounds; or (3) scenario-based discounted cash flow modeling keyed to technology milestones and commercialization timelines specific to photonic quantum computing. The concentration of Series A and later-stage funding in quantum computing, combined with the nascent commercialization landscape, creates structural sparsity in contemporaneous comparables.
